chapter_goal: Make Hopf's holonomy proof inspectable as endpoint comparison, edge cancellation, mesh bookkeeping, and topology balance.
computational_checks: Open-edge R_U changes with U, but not with a constant change of transported initial vector.
Oppositely oriented edge Phi terms cancel.
Closed triangulated meshes have edge incidence two and expected Euler characteristic.
Curvature-index-topology residuals are zero to floating tolerance.
Proof dependency graph is a DAG with the intended theorem path.
All concept-named artifacts exist, are nonzero, and are visually nonblank by pixel statistics.
concept_inventory: Closed-loop holonomy is invariant, but an open-edge rotation needs a comparator field.
The fiducial field U is allowed to be arbitrary because it will cancel from the proof.
Comparing the transported vector with F leaves the index contribution of singularities.
For every polygon, curvature mass minus 2*pi times enclosed index equals a sum of edge Phi terms.
Every interior edge appears twice with opposite orientation, so Phi(K) + Phi(-K) = 0.
A closed triangulated surface has no remaining exterior boundary; topology enters through the index sum.

implementation_notes.copyright_boundary: No source text, screenshots, page crops, or textbook figures are stored.
implementation_notes.write_boundary: Only the assigned chapter notebook and artifacts/chapter-26 are written.

library_routing_table.fallback: Text angle ledger if plotting is unavailable.
library_routing_table.why: Static angle traces make dependence on the comparator field visible and easy to audit numerically.
The proof move is a signed planar incidence diagram rather than a 3D rendering problem.

library_routing_table.library: Trimesh for mesh combinatorics, Matplotlib for the durable artifact
library_routing_table.representation: small closed meshes plus V/E/F table
library_routing_table.why: Trimesh exposes the edge-incidence condition that makes cancellation global.
The theorem is an equality of totals; a residual plot catches sign and index mistakes.
A proof-heavy chapter benefits from explicit dependency structure and reachability checks.

proof_visualization_strategy: Use endpoint comparison, oriented-edge cancellation, mesh ledger, balance plot, and NetworkX DAG.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Compare R_U(K) for two fiducial fields and see that only the closed proof sum is invariant.
visual_sequence.validation: Changing the initial transported vector by a constant angle leaves the open-edge delta unchanged.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Inspect the same shared edge with opposite arrows in neighboring polygons.
Check that each mesh edge is incident to exactly two faces and that chi is computed from V-E+F.
visual_sequence.validation: Sphere chi=2, torus chi=0, all displayed closed-mesh edges have incidence two.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Track total curvature and 2*pi times total index for several closed orientable genera.
visual_sequence.validation: The residual total_curvature - 2*pi*sum(index) vanishes in every row.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: Follow which local definitions are needed before the global theorem appears.
visual_sequence.validation: The graph is acyclic and has a path from open-edge comparison to global Gauss-Bonnet.
